ground_truth: A practical 2025 travel guide to Amsterdam covering transport (Schiphol airport, GVB trams, cycling), must-see museums (Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum, Anne Frank House), neighbourhoods (Jordaan, Amsterdam-Noord, NDSM Wharf), and dining (Café de Jaren, Restaurant Blauw). The guide also notes Amsterdam's overtourism policies under Mayor Femke Halsema, including a €3 tourist tax and Airbnb restrictions.
summary: Amsterdam is a city with an intimate character that offers world-class museums, historic architecture, diverse cuisine, and vibrant nightlife. Getting to Amsterdam is easy by air or train, and cycling is a popular way to explore the city. Must-see museums include the Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum, and Anne Frank House. The Jordaan district is a charming neighbourhood with narrow streets, independent galleries, and vintage shops. Amsterdam has strict policies on tourism, including a tax and restrictions on new shops and Airbnb rentals.
